back_translation: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan River, and the Spirit led him into the wilderness,
for forty days, being tempted by the devil. In those days he ate nothing, and when those days had ended, he was hungry.
Then the devil said to him, 'If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.'
Jesus answered him, saying, 'It is written, "Man shall not live by bread alone."'
Then the devil led him up to a high place and pointed out for him to see all the kingdoms in the world in a single moment.
Then the devil said to him, 'I will give you all the authority and the glory of these kingdoms, because it has been handed over to me, and I will give it to whomever I please.
Therefore if you worship before me, all of this will be yours.'
Jesus answered him, saying, 'It is written, "You shall worship the Lord your God, and serve him only."'
Then the devil led him to Jerusalem and made him stand on the highest pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, 'If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here.
For it is written, "He will command his angels concerning you, to protect you,
and, "They will lift you up in their hands, so that your foot will not strike a stone."'
Jesus answered him, saying, 'It is said, "You shall not test the Lord your God."'
When the devil had finished testing in every way, he left him until a fitting time would come.
Jesus returned to the region of Galilee in the power of the Spirit, and the news about him spread throughout the surrounding region.
He taught in their synagogues, and everyone praised him.
He came to the town of Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue as was his custom, and stood up to read the Scripture.
Someone brought to him the scroll of the prophet Isaiah. He unrolled the scroll and found the passage where it was written:

'The Spirit of the Lord rests upon me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor; he has sent me to proclaim liberation to the captives and to proclaim the recovery of sight to the blind, to proclaim liberation to the oppressed,
to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor.'
Then he rolled up the scroll, returned it to the synagogue attendant, and sat down. The eyes of everyone in the synagogue were fixed on him.
He began to say to them, 'Today this passage of Scripture is fulfilled while you all are listening.'
Everyone spoke well of him and was amazed at the gracious words coming from his mouth, and they said, 'Is this not the son of Joseph?'
He said to them, 'Surely you will quote this proverb to me: "Doctor, heal yourself!" And, "Whatever we have heard you did in the town of Capernaum, do here in your hometown also."'
He continued saying, 'I tell you the truth: no prophet is accepted in his own hometown.
I tell you truly that in the days of Elijah there were many widows in Israel, when the sky was shut for three years and six months, and a great famine occurred throughout all the land,
but God did not send Elijah to any of those widows, except to a foreign widow at the town of Zarephath in the region of Sidon.
And in the days of Elisha the prophet, there were many lepers in Israel, but none of them was cleansed, except Naaman the Syrian.'
When everyone in the synagogue heard these words, they were filled with anger.
They got up, drove him out of the city, and led him to the edge of the cliff of the hill on which their city was built, in order to push him down.
But he passed through the middle of them and went away.
He went down to the town of Capernaum in the region of Galilee, and taught them on the Sabbath day.
They were amazed at his teaching, because his word was full of authority.
In the synagogue there was a man who had an unclean demon spirit possessing him. He cried out in a loud voice, saying:
'Oh! What do we have to do with you, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? We know who you are — the Holy One of God!'
Jesus rebuked it, saying, 'Be quiet and come out of him!' Then the demon threw the man down in the middle of the people, and came out of him without doing him any harm at all.
Astonishment came upon everyone, and they spoke with one another saying, 'What kind of teaching is this? He commands unclean spirits with authority and power, and they come out!'
Therefore the news about him spread throughout every place in that region.
He went out from the synagogue and entered the house of Simon. The mother-in-law of Simon was lying ill with a high fever, so they begged him to help her.
He went and stood over her, rebuked the fever, and the fever left her. Immediately she got up and served him and the others.
When the sun was setting, all those who had people sick with various diseases brought them to him. He laid his hands on them one by one, and healed them.
Demons also came out of many people, crying out, saying, 'You are the Son of God!' But he rebuked them and would not let them speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.
When daybreak came, he went out from the city to a solitary place. The crowds searched for him, came to him, and tried to detain him so that he would not go away from them.
But he said to them, 'I must preach the good news of the kingdom of God to other towns also, because I was sent for this purpose.'
He preached in synagogues throughout the region of Judea.
